label: Audit the current weakness
hypothesisStem: If the current weakness is described precisely, later rounds can target the real failure instead of a surface symptom.
mutationMoves: Name the current failure in one sentence.
List the scene, choice, clue, or convergence pattern causing it.
Translate the weakness into a player-facing failure.
acceptanceAdditions: The weakness is framed as an observable reader or player failure.
The round leaves behind a falsifiable hypothesis.
deliverables: A round brief with a named failure mode.
A before-state note anchored to concrete files.

label: Tighten the contract
hypothesisStem: If the local contract is clearer, the player will understand the threat, the verb set, and the immediate stakes sooner.
mutationMoves: Tighten the promise in opening or transition text.
Clarify what the player is choosing between.
Reduce ambiguity that hides intent rather than building suspense.
acceptanceAdditions: The local scene or segment signals intent before choice.
The revised text preserves suspense while improving legibility.
deliverables: A rewritten opening, transition, or choice prompt.

label: Rewrite a small structural unit
hypothesisStem: If one small structural unit is rewritten instead of patched, the surrounding route will become easier to reason about.
mutationMoves: Replace one brittle node cluster with a cleaner local sequence.
Remove one false branch that adds cost without value.
Add one missing structural beat where the route currently jumps too fast.
acceptanceAdditions: The rewritten unit has a clearer causal shape than before.
The change reduces hidden assumptions between adjacent nodes.
deliverables: A compact rewrite affecting one node family or one route segment.

label: Sharpen choice consequence
hypothesisStem: If option intent and cost are sharper, the interaction will feel more playable and less cosmetic.
mutationMoves: Rewrite choice labels around distinct intentions.
Expose a trust, time, risk, or information tradeoff.
Make at least one consequence visible within one to three later nodes.
acceptanceAdditions: Each choice can be paraphrased as a distinct strategy.
At least one downstream effect becomes visible quickly.
deliverables: One choice set with clearer strategic contrast.

label: Reinforce logic and redundancy
hypothesisStem: If clue and state dependencies are reinforced here, the route will better survive scrutiny and replay.
mutationMoves: Add or reroute one clue path.
Remove one reveal that lacks prerequisite support.
Mark one state change that needs a later payoff.
acceptanceAdditions: No reveal in the touched area depends on unreachable knowledge.
Critical information has at least one visible support path.
deliverables: One dependency note or rewrite that improves fairness.

label: Preserve route memory
hypothesisStem: If convergence preserves route memory instead of flattening it, the story will stay replayable without multiplying content forever.
mutationMoves: Add conditional texture at a merge.
Replace one generic merge with a pressure-based convergence.
Carry a prior action forward through dialogue, access, or suspicion.
acceptanceAdditions: The merge acknowledges meaningful prior play when relevant.
Shared text does not assume facts absent on some incoming paths.
deliverables: One convergence point with stronger path memory.

label: Tune pace and tension
hypothesisStem: If the local rhythm is tuned, the player will feel accumulation instead of repetition or exhaustion.
mutationMoves: Shorten one overlong pressure scene.
Insert one reflection or investigation beat before a burden spike.
Compress one repetitive clue loop into a sharper exchange.
acceptanceAdditions: The touched span has a clearer rise-fall pattern.
The revision reduces boredom without flattening suspense.
deliverables: A pacing adjustment note and one edited scene cluster.

label: Test downstream payoff
hypothesisStem: If downstream payoff is checked early, the route will feel earned when replayed and less hollow on first read.
mutationMoves: Trace one local change into later scenes or endings.
Add one callback line, gate, or reveal response.
Cut one dangling setup that never pays off.
acceptanceAdditions: The touched element now has a later consequence or callback.
The route gains at least one replay-relevant texture.
deliverables: One explicit payoff map or inserted callback.

label: Red-team the route
hypothesisStem: If the route is attacked from a hostile reader viewpoint, hidden contradictions and dead energy will surface before they harden.
mutationMoves: Attack the route for boredom, unfairness, or impossible inference.
Write the strongest skeptical reading of the current logic.
List one change that would break the route and guard against it.
acceptanceAdditions: The round records one adversarial objection and one response.
A contradiction, fairness gap, or dull patch is either fixed or explicitly parked.
deliverables: A red-team note with one defended fix.

label: Codify the gain
hypothesisStem: If the gain is codified now, later rounds can reuse it instead of re-learning the same lesson.
mutationMoves: Write a stable rule for future rounds.
Record what improved and why.
Name the next likely blind spot created by this improvement.
acceptanceAdditions: The round leaves behind one reusable rule.
The checkpoint explains why the band mattered.
deliverables: A checkpoint summary and one next-band handoff note.
